title: EP – Password reset request triggers rate limiting
test_steps:
Send 6 password reset requests within a 1-minute window.
Observe the response on the 6th request.
expected_result: The 6th request returns HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) or equivalent rate-limit error.

title: EP – Login request does not trigger rate limiting
test_steps:
Send 10 login requests within a 1-minute window.
Observe all responses.
expected_result: All requests receive normal authentication responses; none receive rate-limit errors.

title: DT – Apply rate limiting when password reset requested and login not requested
precondition: Rate limit counters are reset; ensure no concurrent login requests
test_steps:
Send a series of password reset requests (7 requests in 2 minutes).
Check the response of the 7th request.
expected_result: The 7th request is blocked with a rate-limit error, demonstrating rate limiting is applied.

title: DT – Do not apply rate limiting when login requested and password reset not requested
precondition: Rate limit counters are reset; ensure no concurrent password reset requests
test_steps:
Send a series of login requests (15 requests in 2 minutes).
Verify all responses.
expected_result: No rate-limit errors occur; all requests are processed without being blocked.

title: State transition path 1 (ALL_TRANSITIONS)
test_steps:
State: Active
State: Active
State: Active
expected_result: Password reset request is rate-limited; login request is not rate-limited.
